title: After the quiz — Mean by Default
body:
**Parent report** — scored 0 of 5 (0%). The trick that caught them most: **Mean by Default**.

Dear Parents,

We've noticed your child is consistently using the average (mean) even when it’s not the best way to describe a set of information; this suggests they are looking for a single, easy answer rather than considering which measure truly represents the data. It seems like they sometimes jump to conclusions without fully thinking through all the possibilities and how different measures might be more appropriate.

**Try at home**

- Collect some cereal boxes from the pantry and have your child estimate their heights – then find the tallest one and compare that to the average height of all the boxes.
- Gather a handful of coins (different denominations are good) and ask them to describe how many there are, trying different ways beyond just adding them up.
- Look at a chart showing favorite ice cream flavors in your family or friends – which way best describes what most people like?
